subject: Project Management
title: Philip Crosby: Quality Is Free
description: A quick, practical guide to Philip Crosby: Quality Is Free.

hook.text: In 1979, Philip Crosby published 'Quality Is Free' — arguing that preventing defects costs less than fixing them.

buildup.text: Crosby challenged the belief that higher quality means higher cost. He said the opposite was true.

discovery.text: The cost of poor quality — rework, scrap, warranty, lost sales — far exceeds the cost of prevention.

twist.text: Crosby's 'zero defects' standard was controversial. Critics said perfection was impossible and demoralizing.

climax.text: His core message endured: investing in prevention isn't an expense — it's the cheapest option.

punchline.text: Doing it right the first time is always cheaper.

quiz.question: What was Philip Crosby's central argument?
options: Preventing defects costs less than fixing them — quality is free
Higher quality always means higher costs
